Looking at the mutant code changes, lines 126-129 were modified. Specifically, in line 127, there was a condition that checked if offset == 0L and returned UTC. Now, it's checking if offset == -1L instead.

By changing the condition in DateTimeZone parsing, maybe this resolved an issue where the wrong time zone was being applied, leading to incorrect month name localization.

However, for the other tests that deal with DST changes and specific date/time calculations, this change didn't affect their outcomes. They continued to fail because their issues aren't directly tied to how offsets are parsed in this particular way. 

So, I think the mutant only fixed the one test it was intended to fix without introducing new issues elsewhere. Therefore, the mutation is adjacent to a fault because it directly addressed the failing test without causing other tests to fail.
